title: USB Loader GX & WiiFlow
requirements: d2x cIOS installed — run the `cios` guide first if you haven't
A USB hard drive or large SD card formatted FAT32 (flash drives are unreliable for this)
warnings: Loaders need cIOS 248-251 to function. If you skipped the cIOS guide, install games will fail or hang at "Waiting for HDD...".
_note: USB Loader GX and WiiFlow (Lite) both load game backups from a USB drive or SD card. Install either or both — WiiFlow leans toward a slicker animated interface with plugin/theme support, USB Loader GX is the older, more configurable option. Both work fine after cIOS is installed.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
This guide installs **USB Loader GX** and **WiiFlow (Lite)** — the two most common Wii game backup loaders. Either lets you launch dumped/backed-up games from a USB drive or SD card instead of a disc.

!!Both require cIOS 248-251 already installed!! — if you haven't done that yet, run the `cios` guide first, or these loaders will hang at "Waiting for HDD..." or fail to launch games.

A large external USB hard drive is strongly recommended for storing games — flash drives tend to be too slow or unreliable for this.

First, I'll check your storage device is FAT32.

steps.title: Put the loaders on your storage device
steps.body: Graphite downloads **USB Loader GX** and **WiiFlow** and unpacks them into the `apps` folder on your storage device. Install both, or just delete the one you don't want from the Homebrew Channel later.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards and USB drives, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull it out.

steps.title: Launch your loader
steps.body:
Plug the storage device into your Wii and power it on. Launch the **Homebrew Channel** and open either **USB Loader GX** or **WiiFlow**.

On first launch, both loaders scan your storage device for games and build their game list. If it's empty, make sure your dumped games (see the `dump-games` guide) are sitting in the `wbfs` or `games` folder the loader expects — each loader's settings menu shows where it's looking.

**WiiFlow** defaults to SD-only search — if your games are on a USB drive instead, change this in **Startup Settings**. Use the bottom-left button to toggle between plugins, games, homebrew, and channels.

steps.title: If a game won't launch
steps.body:
Some games need a specific cIOS slot to run correctly. Both loaders auto-select one, but you can override it if a game crashes or hangs:

- **USB Loader GX**: go to **Settings** → **Game Load** → **Game IOS**, and try slots 248 through 251.
- **WiiFlow**: select the game, press the **gear icon**, and change **cIOS** there.

A compatibility list for tricky games is maintained on GBAtemp if you need to look up the right slot for a specific title.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: You can now launch game backups straight from your USB drive or SD card. Dump more discs with the `dump-games` guide to grow your library, and check `wii-loaders` troubleshooting if a specific game misbehaves.
